text: The laparoscopic view reveals a subhepatic appendix formiformis, observed during the exploration following a cholecystectomy procedure for cholecystitis.
Following the laparoscopic cholecystectomy, the surgeon prepares for a simultaneous laparoscopic appendectomy, focusing on the atypically localized subhepatic appendix formiformis.
The surgeon grasps the appendix and suspends it to expose the mesoappendix, revealing the connective tissue and blood vessels associated with the appendix.
The surgeon dissects the mesoappendix using a ligature, separating it from surrounding tissues in preparation for appendectomy.
The surgeon ligates the arteria appendicularis using a plastic polymer endoclip and then cuts it with a ligature.
The surgeon closes the stump of the appendix with two plastic polymer endoclips and transects the appendix using scissors, completing the appendectomy procedure.
The surgeon places the appendix into a specimen bag and removes it from the abdominal cavity.
